description.value: A high-level summary of how a protein has been modified, e.g. PEGylation, lipidation, Fc fusion. Useful for categorising modified biologics without enumerating the detailed chemistry.

definition.value: Conjugated to one or more polyethylene glycol (PEG) chains for half-life extension. Examples: pegfilgrastim, peginterferon, certolizumab pegol.
Conjugated to a fatty acid chain (acylation) for half-life extension via albumin binding. Examples: semaglutide, liraglutide, insulin detemir, insulin degludec.
Fused to an immunoglobulin Fc region for half-life extension and effector function. Examples: etanercept, abatacept, aflibercept, dulaglutide.
Fused to human serum albumin for half-life extension. Example: albiglutide.
Conjugated to a small-molecule drug payload via a linker (antibody-drug conjugate). Examples: trastuzumab emtansine, brentuximab vedotin, gemtuzumab ozogamicin.
Conjugated to a polysaccharide antigen for vaccine immunogenicity. Examples: Hib, pneumococcal and meningococcal conjugate vaccines.
Glycan structure engineered (e.g. afucosylated) to alter biological activity. Examples: obinutuzumab, mogamulizumab (enhanced ADCC).
Sequence carries one or more amino acid substitutions relative to a natural or parent protein. Examples: insulin analogs lispro, aspart, glargine.
Sequence is shortened relative to the parent protein.
Covalently labelled with biotin. Typical for research or diagnostic reagents.
Covalently labelled with a fluorescent dye. Typical for imaging or flow-cytometry reagents.
